mission.title: cilium: An error occurred when enabling host network mode through Helm
mission.description: An error occurred when enabling host network mode through Helm. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify cilium troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cilium deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review cilium configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cilium configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium
kubectl get configmap -n cilium -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cilium
```
### Is there an existing issue for this?

- [X] I have searched the existing issues

### Version

higher than v1.16.0 and lower than v1.17.0

### What happened?

 helm install cilium cilium -n kube-system
Error: INSTALLATION FAILED: 1 error

steps.title: Apply the fix for An error occurred when enabling host network mode through…
steps.description:
FWIW the following diff applied to the helm chart seems to enable the workaround proposed by .
```diff
diff --git a/install/kubernetes/cilium/templates/validate.yaml b/install/kubernetes/cilium/templates/validate.yaml
index 8bc687dbff..b7101df1a5 100644
---
```yaml
kubeProxyReplacement: true
k8sServiceHost: auto  # Read from kubeadm-config configMap
ingressController:
  enabled: true
  default: true
  enforceHttps: true
  loadbalancerMode: dedicated
  service:
    externalTrafficPolicy: "~"
  # Configure the Envoy listeners to be exposed on the host network.
  hostNetwork:
    enabled: true
```

steps.title: Confirm An error occurred when enabling host network mode… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cilium -n cilium --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cilium --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: FWIW the following diff applied to the helm chart seems to enable the workaround proposed by .
codeSnippets:
kubeProxyReplacement: true
k8sServiceHost: auto  # Read from kubeadm-config configMap
ingressController:
  enabled: true
  default: true
  enforceHttps: true
  loadbalancerMode: dedicated
  service:
    externalTrafficPolicy: "~"
  # Configure the Envoy listeners to be exposed on the host network.
  hostNetwork:
    enabled: true

kubeProxyReplacement: true
k8sServiceHost: auto  # Read from kubeadm-config configMap
ingressController:
  enabled: true
  default: true
  enforceHttps: true
  loadbalancerMode: dedicated
  service:
    # https://github.com/cilium/cilium/issues/34028
    type: ClusterIP
    externalTrafficPolicy: "~"
  # Configure the Envoy listeners to be exposed on the host network.
  hostNetwork:
    enabled: true

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: podinfo
  labels:
    app: podinfo
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 8080
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP
  selector:
    app: podinfo

---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: podinfo
spec:
  ingressClassName: cilium
  rules:
    - host: <host>
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: podinfo
                port:
                  number: 8080

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cilium installed or the issue environment reproducible.
